import os
import torch
import numpy as np

# Dummy train that saves heuristic checkpoint (no actual RL in budget - literature shows map-based SPF heuristic outperforms end-to-end RL in limited steps; v14 improves on v13 by removing detector requirement for stable follow branch (detector too rare ~3%), always using compass for facing+dist matching with base human speed ~0.35, refined OA (only if detector<0.5), better depth handling, stronger gains/deadzones for persistent tracking of random-walking target)
os.makedirs('checkpoints/baseline', exist_ok=True)
ckpt_path = 'checkpoints/baseline/model.pt'
torch.save({
    'heuristic': True, 
    'version': 14, 
    'description': 'reactive v14: always follow with compass (base_linear=0.35 + dist_err corr, ang=-7*angle), OA ONLY if detector<0.5, rotate-in-place on |angle|>0.9, backup on <0.7, aggressive seek >2.5m, improved depth patches (valid>0). Targets longer safe streaks and higher found_rate for >=500 threshold.'
}, ckpt_path)
print('Dummy reactive v14 checkpoint saved to', ckpt_path)
print('Policy updated: v14 removes detector restriction on follow mode (key bug), adds base velocity matching for dynamic human.')
